messages.content: give me 15 bible verses like numbers 31:17-18

Certainly, the following Bible verses bear some similarity to Numbers 31:17-18 in terms of content, theme, or historical context, particularly looking at aspects of war, judgment, and God's commands in the Old Testament. Keep in mind that Numbers 31:17-18 details Israel's treatment of the Midianite women and children following God's command to Moses after a battle, which is a very specific and controversial passage, and similar verses might also involve warfare, divine instructions, or moral decrees.

Here are 15 verses that share some thematic commonalities:

1. **Deuteronomy 20:16-17**
   "But in the cities of the nations the LORD your God is giving you as an inheritance, do not leave alive anything that breathes. Completely destroy them—the Hittites, Amorites, Canaanites, Perizzites, Hivites and Jebusites—as the LORD your God has commanded you."

2. **1 Samuel 15:3**
   "Now go, attack the Amalekites and totally destroy all that belongs to them. Do not spare them; put to death men and women, children and infants, cattle and sheep, camels and donkeys."

3. **Deuteronomy 21:10-13**
   "When you go to war against your enemies and the LORD your God delivers them into your hands and you take captives, if you notice among the captives a beautiful woman and are attracted to her, you may take her as your wife. Bring her into your home and have her shave her head, trim her nails and put aside the clothes she was wearing when captured. After she has lived in your house and mourned her father and mother for a full month, then you may go to her and be her husband and she shall be your wife."

4. **Joshua 6:21**
   "They devoted the city to the LORD and destroyed with the sword every living thing in it—men and women, young and old, cattle, sheep and donkeys."

5. **Exodus 32:27-28**
   "He said to them, 'This is what the LORD, the God of Israel, says: "Each man strap a sword to his side. Go back and forth through the camp from one end to the other, each killing his brother and friend and neighbor."' The Levites did as Moses commanded, and that day about three thousand of the people died."

6. **Deuteronomy 2:34**
   "At that time we took all his towns and completely destroyed them—men, women and children. We left no survivors."

7. **Deuteronomy 3:6**
   "We completely destroyed them, as we did to Sihon king of Heshbon, destroying every city—men, women and children."

8. **Joshua 10:40**
   "So Joshua subdued the whole region, including the hill country, the Negev, the western foothills and the mountain slopes, together with all their kings. He left no survivors. He totally destroyed all who breathed, just as the LORD, the God of Israel, had commanded."

9. **Judges 21:10-12**
   "So the assembly sent twelve thousand fighting men with instructions to go to Jabesh Gilead and put to the sword those living there, including the women and children. 'This is what you are to do,' they said. 'Kill every male and every woman who is not a virgin.' They found among the people living in Jabesh Gilead four hundred young women who had never slept with a man, and they took them to the camp at Shiloh in Canaan."

10. **Ezekiel 9:5-6**
    "As I listened, he said to the others, 'Follow him through the city and kill, without showing pity or compassion. Slaughter the old men, the young men and women, the mothers and children, but do not touch anyone who has the mark. Begin at my sanctuary.' So they began with the old men who were in front of the temple."

11. **Joshua 8:24-25**
    "When Israel had finished killing all the men of Ai in the fields and in the wilderness where they had chased them, and when every one of them had been put to the sword, all the Israelites returned to Ai and killed those who were in it. Twelve thousand men and women fell that day—all the people of Ai."

12. **1 Samuel 27:8-9**
    "Now David and his men went up and raided the Geshurites, the Girzites and the Amalekites. From ancient times these peoples had lived in the land extending to Shur and Egypt. Whenever David attacked an area, he did not leave a man or woman alive, but he took sheep and cattle, donkeys and camels, and clothes. Then he returned to Achish."

13. **2 Chronicles 25:12**
    "The Judean army captured 10,000 men alive, took them to the top of a cliff and threw them down so that all were dashed to pieces."

14. **Leviticus 26:7-8**
    "You will chase your enemies, and they will fall by the sword before you. Five of you will chase a hundred, and a hundred of you will chase ten thousand, and your enemies will fall by the sword before you."

15. **Deuteronomy 7:2**
    "And when the LORD your God has delivered them over to you and you have defeated them, then you must destroy them totally. Make no treaty with them, and show them no mercy."

It is important to note that these passages reflect a different historical and cultural context that is far removed from modern sensibilities. Interpreting these passages requires understanding their place within the broader narrative of the Bible as well as examining the historical and theological context. Some of these passages are difficult and have led to various discussions and interpretations among theologians and scholars.
give me more

Of course, here are additional Bible verses that involve commands or actions related to warfare and judgement, akin to the nature of Numbers 31:17-18:

16. **Joshua 11:11-12**
   "Everyone in it they put to the sword. They totally destroyed them, not sparing anyone that breathed, and he burned Hazor itself. Joshua took all these royal cities and their kings and put them to the sword. He totally destroyed them, as Moses the servant of the LORD had commanded."

17. **Deuteronomy 7:23-24**
   "But the LORD your God will deliver them over to you, throwing them into great confusion until they are destroyed. He will give their kings into your hand, and you will wipe out their names from under heaven. No one will be able to stand up against you; you will destroy them."

18. **Judges 18:27-28**
   "Then they took what Micah had made, and his priest, and went on to Laish, against a people at peace and secure. They attacked them with the sword and burned down their city. There was no one to rescue them because it was far from Sidon and they had no dealings with anyone; it was in the valley near Beth Rehob. The Danites rebuilt the city and settled there."

19. **Jeremiah 50:21-22**
   "Attack the land of Merathaim and those who live in Pekod. Pursue, kill and completely destroy them," declares the LORD. "Do everything I have commanded you. The noise of battle is in the land, the noise of great destruction!"

20. **Ezekiel 9:5-7**
   "To the others he said in my hearing, 'Go after him through the city and kill, without showing pity or compassion. Slaughter the old men, the young men and women, the mothers and children, but do not touch anyone who has the mark. Begin at my sanctuary.' So they began with the elders who were in front of the temple."

21. **1 Samuel 22:18-19**
   "The king then ordered Doeg, 'You turn and strike down the priests.' So Doeg the Edomite turned and struck them down. That day he killed eighty-five men who wore the linen ephod. He also put to the sword Nob, the town of the priests, with its men and women, its children and infants, and its cattle, donkeys and sheep."

22. **2 Kings 10:25**
   "As soon as Jehu had finished making the burnt offering, he ordered the guards and officers: 'Go in and kill them; let no one escape.' So they cut them down with the sword. The guards and officers threw the bodies out and then entered the inner shrine of the temple of Baal."

23. **Ezekiel 35:7-9**
   "I will make Mount Seir a desolate waste and cut off from it all who come and go. I will fill your mountains with the slain; those killed by the sword will fall on your hills and in your valleys and in all your ravines. I will make you desolate forever; your towns will not be inhabited. Then you will know that I am the LORD."

24. **2 Chronicles 20:23**
   "The men of Ammon and Moab rose up against the men from Mount Seir to destroy and annihilate them. After they finished slaughtering the men from Seir, they helped to destroy one another."

25. **Joshua 10:28**
   "That day Joshua took Makkedah. He put the city and its king to the sword and totally destroyed everyone in it. He left no survivors. And he did to the king of Makkedah as he had done to the king of Jericho."

26. **Joshua 8:26-27**
   "Joshua did not draw back the hand that held out his javelin until he had destroyed all who lived in Ai. But Israel did carry off for themselves the livestock and plunder of this city, as the LORD had instructed Joshua."

27. **2 Kings 8:12**
   "Hazael said, 'Why is my lord weeping?' He answered, 'Because I know the evil you will do to the Israelites: You will set fire to their fortified places, kill their young men with the sword, dash their little children to the ground, and rip open their pregnant women.'"

28. **Hosea 13:16**
   "The people of Samaria must bear their guilt, because they have rebelled against their God. They will fall by the sword; their little ones will be dashed to the ground, their pregnant women ripped open."

29. **1 Chronicles 4:41**
   "These men whose names were recorded came in the days of Hezekiah king of Judah. They attacked the Hamites in their dwellings and also the Meunites who were there and completely destroyed them, as is evident to this day. Then they settled in their place, because there was pasture for their flocks."

30. **Numbers 21:34-35**
   "The LORD said to Moses, 'Do not be afraid of him, for I have delivered him into your hands, along with his whole army and his land. Do to him what you did to Sihon king of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon.' So they struck him down, together with his sons and his whole army, leaving no survivors. And they took possession of his land."

These verses represent a bygone era and reflect an ancient perspective on issues like warfare and divine justice. While they are part of the biblical canon, their direct application to contemporary life and ethics is complex and should be carefully considered within a broad theological and historical framework.
give me more

Here are additional verses from the Old Testament that align with the themes of warfare, divine judgment, or the carrying out of God’s commands similar to those in Numbers 31:17-18:

31. **Numbers 25:16-17**
    "The Lord said to Moses, 'Treat the Midianites as enemies and kill them.'"

32. **Deuteronomy 20:13-14**
    "When the Lord your God delivers it into your hand, put to the sword all the men in it. As for the women, the children, the livestock and everything else in the city, you may take these as plunder for yourselves. And you may use the plunder the Lord your God gives you from your enemies."

33. **Judges 1:8**
    "The sons of Judah fought against Jerusalem and captured it and struck it with the edge of the sword and set the city on fire."

34. **Judges 12:6**
    "Then they [Gileadites] said to him, 'Say now, "Shibboleth." But he said, "Sibboleth," for he could not pronounce it correctly. Then they seized him and slew him at the fords of the Jordan. Thus there fell at that time 42,000 of Ephraim."

35. **Judges 21:11**
    "This is what you are to do, 'completely destroy every male and every woman who is not a virgin.'"

36. **Joshua 6:17**
    "The city and all that is in it are to be devoted to the Lord. Only Rahab the prostitute and all who are with her in her house shall be spared, because she hid the spies we sent."

37. **Joshua 10:29-30**
    "Then Joshua and all Israel with him moved on from Makkedah to Libnah and attacked it. And the Lord also gave that city and its king into the hands of Israel. He took it on the second day and put it and everyone in it to the sword, just as he had done to Lachish."

38. **1 Samuel 15:8-9**
    "He took Agag king of the Amalekites alive, and all his people he totally destroyed with the sword. But Saul and the army spared Agag and the best of the sheep and cattle, the fat calves and lambs—everything that was good. These they were unwilling to destroy completely, but everything that was despised and weak they totally destroyed."

39. **2 Kings 2:23-24**
    "From there Elisha went up to Bethel. As he was walking along the road, some boys came out of the town and jeered at him. 'Go on up, you baldhead!' they said. 'Go on up, you baldhead!' He turned around, looked at them and called down a curse on them in the name of the Lord. Then two bears came out of the woods and mauled forty-two of the boys."

40. **2 Kings 19:35**
    "That night the angel of the Lord went out and put to death a hundred and eighty-five thousand in the Assyrian camp. When the people got up the next morning—there were all the dead bodies!"

41. **2 Chronicles 25:12**
    "The army of Judah captured ten thousand men alive, took them to the top of a cliff and threw them down so that all were dashed to pieces."

42. **Job 1:15**
    "And the Sabeans fell upon them and took them and struck down the servants with the edge of the sword, and I alone have escaped to tell you."

43. **Ezekiel 32:11-12**
    "For this is what the Sovereign Lord says: 'The sword of the king of Babylon will come against you. I will cause your hordes to fall by the swords of mighty men—the most ruthless of all nations. They will shatter the pride of Egypt, and all her hordes will be overthrown.'"

44. **Habakkuk 3:13**
    "You came out to deliver your people, to save your anointed one. You crushed the leader of the land of wickedness, you stripped him from head to foot."

45. **Zechariah 14:2**
    "I will gather all the nations to Jerusalem to fight against it; the city will be captured, the houses ransacked, and the women raped. Half of the city will go into exile, but the rest of the people will not be taken from the city."

These verses provide different portrayals of violence and warfare in various historical and theological settings within the Bible. Modern readers often wrestle with these accounts, seeking ways to reconcile such narratives with contemporary spiritual and ethical paradigms. It's important for readers to consider these texts through scholarly study and within the context of their original audiences and surrounding canonical material.
give me more

Here are additional verses from the Bible with themes related to war, destruction, and God's commands:

46. **Numbers 33:52-53**
   "you shall drive out all the inhabitants of the land from before you and destroy all their figured stones and destroy all their metal images and demolish all their high places. And you shall take possession of the land and settle in it, for I have given the land to you to possess it."

47. **Deuteronomy 12:2-3**
   "You shall surely destroy all the places where the nations whom you shall dispossess served their gods, on the high mountains and on the hills and under every green tree. You shall tear down their altars and dash in pieces their pillars and burn their Asherim with fire. You shall chop down the carved images of their gods and destroy their name out of that place."

48. **Deuteronomy 13:15**
   "you shall surely strike the inhabitants of that city with the edge of the sword, destroying it utterly and all that is in it and its livestock with the edge of the sword."

49. **Joshua 10:32**
   "And the Lord gave Lachish into the hand of Israel, and he captured it on the second day, and struck it and all the people who were in it with the edge of the sword, just as he had done to Libnah."

50. **Joshua 11:14**
   "And all the spoil of these cities and the livestock, the people of Israel took for their plunder. But every person they struck with the edge of the sword until they had destroyed them, and they did not leave any who breathed."

51. **Judges 7:24-25**
   "Gideon sent messengers throughout all the hill country of Ephraim, saying, 'Come down against the Midianites and capture the waters against them, as far as Beth-barah, and also the Jordan.' So all the men of Ephraim were called out, and they captured the waters as far as Beth-barah, and also the Jordan. And they captured the two princes of Midian, Oreb and Zeeb; and they killed Oreb at the rock of Oreb, and Zeeb they killed at the winepress of Zeeb. Then they pursued Midian, and they brought the heads of Oreb and Zeeb to Gideon across the Jordan."

52. **Judges 16:30**
   "And Samson said, 'Let me die with the Philistines.' Then he bowed with all his strength, and the house fell upon the lords and upon all the people who were in it. So the dead whom he killed at his death were more than those whom he had killed during his life."

53. **1 Samuel 6:19**
   "He struck some of the men of Beth-shemesh, because they had looked upon the ark of the Lord. He struck seventy men of them, and the people mourned because the Lord had struck the people with a great blow."

54. **1 Samuel 15:18-19**
   "And the Lord sent you on a mission and said, 'Go, devote to destruction the sinners, the Amalekites, and fight against them until they are consumed.' Why then did you not obey the voice of the Lord? Why did you pounce on the spoil and do what was evil in the sight of the Lord?"

55. **1 Kings 18:40**
   "And Elijah said to them, 'Seize the prophets of Baal; let not one of them escape.' And they seized them. And Elijah brought them down to the brook Kishon and slaughtered them there."

56. **1 Kings 20:42**
   "And he said to him, 'Thus says the Lord, "Because you have let go out of your hand the man whom I had devoted to destruction, therefore your life shall be for his life, and your people for his people."'"

57. **2 Chronicles 32:21**
   "And the Lord sent an angel, who cut off all the mighty warriors and commanders and officers in the camp of the king of Assyria. So he returned with shame of face to his own land. And when he came into the house of his god, some of his own sons struck him down there with the sword."

58. **Isaiah 13:15-16**
   "Whoever is found will be thrust through, and whoever is caught will fall by the sword. Their infants will be dashed in pieces before their eyes; their houses will be plundered and their wives ravished."

59. **Isaiah 34:2-3**
   "For the Lord is enraged against all the nations, and furious against all their host; he has devoted them to destruction, has given them over for slaughter. Their slain shall be cast out, and the stench of their corpses shall rise; the mountains shall flow with their blood."

60. **Jeremiah 25:33**
   "And those pierced by the Lord on that day shall extend from one end of the earth to the other. They shall not be lamented, or gathered, or buried; they shall be dung on the surface of the ground."

These verses, drawn from different parts of the Old Testament, illustrate the context-specific nature of God's commands regarding warfare and judgment during the times in which the texts were written. Interpretations and applications of these passages require thoughtful reflection and consideration of the overall message of the Bible.
give me 10 bible verses on  religious intolerance.

Religious intolerance isn't explicitly promoted in the Bible, but there are several passages where either God or His followers take actions against other religious practices that could be perceived as expressions of a monotheistic or exclusive religious view. Here are 10 verses from the Bible that can be related to the concept of religious intolerance primarily in terms of the Biblical directive for exclusive worship of Yahweh and the denunciation of idolatry:

1. **Exodus 20:3-5 (NIV)**
   "You shall have no other gods before me. You shall not make for yourself an image in the form of anything in heaven above or on the earth beneath or in the waters below. You shall not bow down to them or worship them; for I, the Lord your God, am a jealous God..."

2. **Deuteronomy 13:6-9 (NIV)**
   "If your very own brother, or your son or daughter, or the wife you love, or your closest friend secretly entices you, saying, ‘Let us go and worship other gods’... do not yield to them or listen to them. Show them no pity. Do not spare them or shield them. You must certainly put them to death..."

3. **1 Kings 18:40 (NIV)**
   "Then Elijah commanded them, 'Seize the prophets of Baal. Don’t let anyone get away!' They seized them, and Elijah had them brought down to the Kishon Valley and slaughtered there."

4. **2 Chronicles 15:12-13 (NIV)**
   "They entered into a covenant to seek the Lord, the God of their ancestors, with all their heart and soul. All who would not seek the Lord, the God of Israel, were to be put to death, whether small or great, man or woman."

5. **Exodus 22:20 (NIV)**
   "Whoever sacrifices to any god other than the Lord must be destroyed."

6. **Deuteronomy 7:5 (NIV)**
   "This is what you are to do to them: Break down their altars, smash their sacred stones, cut down their Asherah poles and burn their idols in the fire."

7. **2 Kings 17:29 (NIV)**
   "Nevertheless, each national group made its own gods in the several towns where they settled, and set them up in the shrines the people of Samaria had made at the high places."

8. **Ezra 6:21 (NIV)**
   "So the Israelites who had returned from the exile ate it, together with all who had separated themselves from the unclean practices of their Gentile neighbors in order to seek the Lord, the God of Israel."

9. **2 Kings 23:24 (NIV)**
   "Furthermore, Josiah got rid of the mediums and spiritists, the household gods, the idols and all the other detestable things seen in Judah and Jerusalem. This he did to fulfill the requirements of the law written in the book that Hilkiah the priest had discovered in the temple of the Lord."

10. **Ezra 10:11 (NIV)**
    "Now honor the Lord, the God of your ancestors, and do his will. Separate yourselves from the peoples around you and from your foreign wives."

These verses often reflect historical circumstances where the Israelites were commanded to maintain purity in their worship and to avoid adopting the religious practices of neighbouring peoples. They represent Biblical directives within a specific covenant context and were part of the religious laws and narratives about the Israelite identity at that time. In the New Testament, the approach to people of other faiths tends to focus more on the spiritual transformation through faith in Christ rather than physical actions against non-believers.

Please be aware that the concept and attitudes toward religious tolerance has evolved significantly since the time when these texts were written. Interpretation of these verses needs to consider the larger Biblical narrative, including themes of love, acceptance, and respect that are found throughout the scriptures.
give me 15 bible verses like Deuteronomy 13:6-9

Deuteronomy 13:6-9 deals with the protection of the Israelite community's purity of worship by commanding the Israelites to reject any enticement to idolatry, even if it comes from within one's own close circle, and prescribing severe consequences for such actions. Here are 15 other Bible verses that contain themes of idolatry, the strictness of God's laws against it, and, in some cases, commands to deal with idolaters:

1. **Deuteronomy 13:12-15**
   "If you hear it said about one of the towns the Lord your God is giving you to live in, that troublemakers have arisen among you and have led the people of their town astray, saying, 'Let us go and worship other gods' (gods you have not known), then you must inquire, probe and investigate it thoroughly. And if it is true and it has been proved that this detestable thing has been done among you, you must certainly put to the sword all who live in that town."

2. **Exodus 22:20**
   "Whoever sacrifices to any god other than the Lord must be destroyed."

3. **Leviticus 20:2**
   "Say to the Israelites: ‘Any Israelite or any foreigner residing in Israel who sacrifices any of his children to Molek is to be put to death. The members of the community are to stone him.'"

4. **Deuteronomy 17:2-5**
   "If a man or woman living among you in one of the towns the Lord gives you is found doing evil in the eyes of the Lord your God in violation of his covenant, and contrary to my command has worshiped other gods, bowing down to them or to the sun or the moon or the stars in the sky... then you must take the man or woman who has done this evil deed to your city gate and stone that person to death."

5. **Deuteronomy 12:2-3**
   "Destroy completely all the places where the nations you are dispossessing worship their gods, on the high mountains, on the hills, and under every spreading tree. Break down their altars, smash their sacred stones and burn their Asherah poles in the fire; cut down the idols of their gods and wipe out their names from those places."

6. **Deuteronomy 18:10**
   "Let no one be found among you who sacrifices their son or daughter in the fire, who practices divination or sorcery, interprets omens, engages in witchcraft."

7. **Joshua 23:16**
   "If you violate the covenant of the Lord your God, which he commanded you, and go and serve other gods and bow down to them, the Lord’s anger will burn against you, and you will quickly perish from the good land he has given you."

8. **2 Chronicles 15:12-13**
   "They entered into a covenant to seek the Lord, the God of their ancestors, with all their heart and soul. All who would not seek the Lord, the God of Israel, were to be put to death, whether small or great, man or woman."

9. **1 Corinthians 10:14**
   "Therefore, my dear friends, flee from idolatry."

10. **1 Kings 18:40**
    "Then Elijah said to them, 'Seize the prophets of Baal. Don’t let anyone get away!' They seized them, and Elijah had them brought down to the Kishon Valley and slaughtered there."

11. **Numbers 25:4-5**
    "The Lord said to Moses, 'Take all the leaders of these people, kill them and expose them in broad daylight before the Lord, so that the Lord's fierce anger may turn away from Israel.' So Moses said to Israel's judges, 'Each of you must put to death those of your people who have yoked themselves to the Baal of Peor.'"

12. **2 Kings 10:25**
    "As soon as Jehu had finished making the burnt offering, he ordered the guards and officers: 'Go in and kill them; let no one escape.' So they cut them down with the sword. The guards and officers threw the bodies out and then entered the inner shrine of the temple of Baal."

13. **Ezekiel 20:7-8**
    "And I said to them: 'Each of you, get rid of the vile images you have set your eyes on, and do not defile yourselves with the idols of Egypt. I am the Lord your God.' But they rebelled against me and would not listen to me; they did not get rid of the vile images they had set their eyes on, nor did they forsake the idols of Egypt."

14. **Acts 17:29-30**
    "Therefore since we are God's offspring, we should not think that the divine being is like gold or silver or stone—an image made by man's design and skill. In the past God overlooked such ignorance, but now he commands all people everywhere to repent."

15. **Revelation 21:8**
    "But the cowardly, the unbelieving, the vile, the murderers, the sexually immoral, those who practice magic arts, the idolaters and all liars—they will be consigned to the fiery lake of burning sulfur. This is the second death."

Keep in mind that while the Old Testament accounts are reflective of a specific historical context and covenant relationship between God and the nation of Israel, the New Testament provides a different perspective focused on spiritual transformation, repentance, and the internal rejection of idolatry without prescribing physical violence against idolaters. Therefore, the way these texts are interpreted and applied in contemporary faith communities varies widely and should be approached with cultural and historical understanding.
give me 15 bible verses about apostacy

Apostasy refers to the act of renouncing or abandoning one's faith or religious beliefs. The Bible, particularly the Old Testament, includes a number of verses that deal with the Israelites turning away from God, which is considered apostasy. Here are 15 verses that address this topic:

1. **Deuteronomy 13:6-9 (NIV)**
   "If your very own brother, or your son or daughter, or the wife you love, or your closest friend secretly entices you, saying, ‘Let us go and worship other gods’... do not yield to them or listen to them. Show them no pity. Do not spare them or shield them. You must certainly put them to death. Your hand must be the first in putting them to death, and then the hands of all the people."

2. **Hebrews 6:4-6 (NIV)**
   "For it is impossible for those who have once been enlightened, who have tasted the heavenly gift, who have shared in the Holy Spirit, who have tasted the goodness of the word of God and the powers of the coming age and who have fallen away, to be brought back to repentance. To their loss they are crucifying the Son of God all over again and subjecting him to public disgrace."

3. **2 Peter 2:20-21 (NIV)**
   "If they have escaped the corruption of the world by knowing our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ and are again entangled in it and are overcome, they are worse off at the end than they were at the beginning. It would have been better for them not to have known the way of righteousness, than to have known it and then to turn their backs on the sacred command that was passed on to them."

4. **1 Timothy 4:1 (NIV)**
   "The Spirit clearly says that in later times some will abandon the faith and follow deceiving spirits and things taught by demons."

5. **Luke 8:13 (NIV)**
   "Those on the rocky ground are the ones who receive the word with joy when they hear it, but they have no root. They believe for a while, but in the time of testing they fall away."

6. **2 Thessalonians 2:3 (NIV)**
   "Don’t let anyone deceive you in any way, for that day will not come until the rebellion occurs and the man of lawlessness is revealed, the man doomed to destruction."

7. **Galatians 5:4 (NIV)**
   "You who are trying to be justified by the law have been alienated from Christ; you have fallen away from grace."

8. **2 Timothy 4:3-4 (NIV)**
   "For the time will come when people will not put up with sound doctrine. Instead, to suit their own desires, they will gather around them a great number of teachers to say what their itching ears want to hear. They will turn their ears away from the truth and turn aside to myths."

9. **Ezekiel 18:24 (NIV)**
   "But if a righteous person turns from their righteousness and commits sin and does the same detestable things the wicked person does, will they live? None of the righteous things that person has done will be remembered. Because of the unfaithfulness they are guilty of and because of the sins they have committed, they will die."

10. **Matthew 24:10-12 (NIV)**
    "At that time many will turn away from the faith and will betray and hate each other, and many false prophets will appear and deceive many people. Because of the increase of wickedness, the love of most will grow cold."

11. **Jeremiah 17:5-6 (NIV)**
    "This is what the Lord says: 'Cursed is the one who trusts in man, who draws strength from mere flesh and whose heart turns away from the Lord. That person will be like a bush in the wastelands; they will not see prosperity when it comes. They will dwell in the parched places of the desert, in a salt land where no one lives.'"

12. **Hosea 14:4 (NIV)**
    "I will heal their waywardness and love them freely, for my anger has turned away from them."

13. **2 Chronicles 15:2 (NIV)**
    "He went out to meet Asa and said to him, ‘Listen to me, Asa and all Judah and Benjamin. The Lord is with you when you are with him. If you seek him, he will be found by you, but if you forsake him, he will forsake you.'"

14. **Galatians 1:6 (NIV)**
    "I am astonished that you are so quickly deserting the one who called you to live in the grace of Christ and are turning to a different gospel—"

15. **Proverbs 14:14 (NIV)**
    "The backslider in heart will be filled with his own ways, but a good man will be satisfied from above."

These verses cover a range of responses to apostasy, from warnings to the consequences faced by those who turn away. They are drawn from both the New and Old Testaments, showing different aspects of how apostasy was viewed and dealt with in Biblical times. Interpretations of these passages must take into account the broader context of the Bible as a whole, including the New Testament's teachings on grace, forgiveness, and reconciliation.
give me 15 bible verses like Ezekiel 18:24

Ezekiel 18:24 addresses the theme of righteousness and its loss due to turning to sin. This passage emphasizes that past righteousness doesn't guarantee future favor if one turns to iniquity. Here are 15 verses that discuss similar themes of accountability, the fleeting nature of righteousness when followed by sin, and consequences of turning away from God:

1. **Ezekiel 18:26 (NIV)**
   "If a righteous person turns from their righteousness and commits sin, they will die for it; because of the sin they have committed they will die."

2. **Ezekiel 33:18 (NIV)**
   "If a righteous person turns from their righteousness and does evil, they will die for it."

3. **Hebrews 10:26-27 (NIV)**
   "If we deliberately keep on sinning after we have received the knowledge of the truth, no sacrifice for sins is left, but only a fearful expectation of judgment and of raging fire that will consume the enemies of God."

4. **2 Peter 2:20-22 (NIV)**
   "If they have escaped the corruption of the world by knowing our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ and are again entangled in it and are overcome, they are worse off at the end than they were at the beginning."

5. **Proverbs 21:16 (NIV)**
   "Whoever strays from the path of prudence comes to rest in the company of the dead."

6. **Jeremiah 17:5 (NIV)**
   "This is what the Lord says: 'Cursed is the one who trusts in man, who depends on flesh for his strength and whose heart turns away from the Lord.'"

7. **Galatians 5:4 (ESV)**
   "You are severed from Christ, you who would be justified by the law; you have fallen away from grace."

8. **Matthew 24:12 (NIV)**
   "Because of the increase of wickedness, the love of most will grow cold."

9. **Romans 11:20-22 (NIV)**
   "Do not be arrogant, but tremble. For if God did not spare the natural branches, he will not spare you either. Consider therefore the kindness and sternness of God: sternness to those who fell, but kindness to you, provided that you continue in his kindness. Otherwise, you also will be cut off."

10. **Luke 8:13 (NIV)**
    "Those on the rocky ground are the ones who receive the word with joy when they hear it, but they have no root. They believe for a while, but in the time of testing they fall away."

11. **Hebrews 6:4-6 (ESV)**
    "For it is impossible, in the case of those who have once been enlightened, who have tasted the heavenly gift, and have shared in the Holy Spirit, and have tasted the goodness of the word of God and the powers of the age to come, and then have fallen away, to restore them again to repentance, since they are crucifying once again the Son of God to their own harm and holding him up to contempt."

12. **James 5:19-20 (NIV)**
    "My brothers and sisters, if one of you should wander from the truth and someone should bring that person back, remember this: Whoever turns a sinner from the error of their way will save them from death and cover over a multitude of sins."

13. **1 Corinthians 9:27 (NIV)**
    "No, I strike a blow to my body and make it my slave so that after I have preached to others, I myself will not be disqualified for the prize."

14. **2 Chronicles 15:2 (NIV)**
    "He went out to meet Asa and said to him, 'Listen to me, Asa and all Judah and Benjamin. The Lord is with you when you are with him. If you seek him, he will be found by you, but if you forsake him, he will forsake you.'"

15. **Hosea 6:4 (ESV)**
    "What shall I do with you, O Ephraim? What shall I do with you, O Judah? Your love is like a morning cloud, like the dew that goes early away."

Each of these verses reinforces the concept found in Ezekiel that a person's past righteousness cannot atone for their current sinful acts, making it imperative to maintain a consistent and faithful walk with God. The New Testament offers more emphasis on God's grace in Christ and the importance of faith, but still warns against falling away after being exposed to the truth of the Gospel.

give me 15 bible verses like proverbs 28:9

Proverbs 28:9 states, "If anyone turns a deaf ear to my instruction, even their prayers are detestable." This verse conveys the principle that ignoring God's commandments leads to a severance of communication with God, where even prayers are not received favorably. Here are 15 other Bible verses that touch on similar themes such as the importance of obedience, the consequences of ignoring God's commands, and the relationship between sin and prayer:

1. **Psalm 66:18 (NIV)**
   "If I had cherished sin in my heart, the Lord would not have listened."

2. **Isaiah 59:2 (NIV)**
    "But your iniquities have separated you from your God; your sins have hidden his face from you, so that he will not hear."

3. **James 4:3 (NIV)**
   "When you ask, you do not receive, because you ask with wrong motives, that you may spend what you get on your pleasures."

4. **1 John 3:22 (NIV)**
   "And whatever we ask we receive from him, because we keep his commandments and do what pleases him."

5. **Proverbs 15:29 (NIV)**
   "The LORD is far from the wicked, but he hears the prayer of the righteous."

6. **John 9:31 (NIV)**
   "We know that God does not listen to sinners. He listens to the godly person who does his will."

7. **Micah 3:4 (NIV)**
   "Then they will cry out to the LORD, but he will not answer them. At that time he will hide his face from them because of the evil they have done."

8. **Zechariah 7:13 (NIV)**
   "'When I called, they did not listen; so when they called, I would not listen,' says the LORD Almighty."

9. **1 Samuel 28:6 (NIV)**
   "He inquired of the LORD, but the LORD did not answer him by dreams or Urim or prophets."

10. **Lamentations 3:44 (NIV)**
    "You have covered yourself with a cloud so that no prayer can get through."

11. **Hosea 5:6 (NIV)**
    "When they go with their flocks and herds to seek the LORD, they will not find him; he has withdrawn himself from them."

12. **Psalm 18:41 (NIV)**
    "They cried for help, but there was no one to save them—to the LORD, but he did not answer."

13. **Proverbs 1:28-29 (NIV)**
    "Then they will call to me but I will not answer; they will look for me but will not find me, since they hated knowledge and did not choose to fear the LORD."

14. **Job 27:9 (NIV)**
    "Will God listen to their cry when distress comes upon them?"

15. **Isaiah 1:15 (NIV)**
    "When you spread out your hands in prayer, I hide my eyes from you; even when you offer many prayers, I am not listening. Your hands are full of blood!"

These verses throughout the Old and New Testaments underline a biblical principle that obedience to God's will is closely tied to the efficacy of prayer and God's willingness to listen. The recurring theme is that living a life contrary to God's commandments may hinder one's prayers from being answered. They emphasize the importance of repentance and aligning one's actions with God’s decrees.
